value:
Operationalization plan for the seed-density principle. See conventions.seedDensity for the principle itself. Three layers, in increasing cost order. Don't build the later layers before validating the earlier ones — convention before infrastructure.

LAYER 1 — Bake the principle into the codex itself.
Status: DONE 2026-04-09. Saved as conventions.seedDensity. CLAUDE.md updated. Future agents read it on bootstrap and write entries with the activation-per-byte lens.

LAYER 2 — Build tools that operationalize it.
File as v1.12+ GitHub issues. Don't build until Layer 1 has shaped real entry writing.

  A. ccli lint --seed-quality — heuristic detection of low-amplification entries. Leans on existing arch.lint infrastructure.
  B. ccli topology (or ccli graph) — co-occurrence analysis on audit.jsonl. Surface which entries get pulled in the same sessions. Killer screenshot for dogfooding post.

LAYER 3 — North-star ideas (v1.13+).
Lens: "does this help users write seeds that activate denser biomes, or does it just add storage capacity?"
  - Telemetry-driven tier promotion in codex_context
  - Seed-pack templates for ccli init
  - Optional meta.testedWith: <model-id> for generator-version drift
  - ccli explain <entry> — LLM self-introspection on what biome an entry selects

CAVEATS: Don't measure LLM activation directly. Don't make seed-density a hard requirement. Don't build Layer 2 before Layer 1 proves out. Treat as lens, not rule.
